ogTitle: Sackville vs Saddleback Mountain: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Sackville and Saddleback Mountain.
heading: Sackville vs Saddleback Mountain — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Sackville, New South Wales and Saddleback Mountain, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Sackville has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Saddleback Mountain (46/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Sackville or Saddleback Mountain safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Sackville has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Saddleback Mountain scores 41/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Sackville scores 20/100 for bushfire risk. Saddleback Mountain scores 56/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Sackville or Saddleback Mountain?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Sackville has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 2.35% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
